name: EC2 Instances Should Not Allow Metadata Response Hop Limit Higher Than 1
description: EC2 Instances allowing metadata hop count higher than 1
body:
### Security intent

This control hardens workloads by ensuring that **eC2 Instances Should Not Allow Metadata Response Hop Limit Higher Than 1**.

### Workload state reviewed

The assessment reviews eC2 Instances allowing metadata hop count higher than 1 in AWS. It focuses on the workload, runtime, image, role, network, or platform setting relevant to this control.

### Remediation focus

Treat compute findings as workload changes. Confirm ownership, update infrastructure as code or deployment templates, roll out in batches where needed, and validate that new resources inherit the corrected baseline.
remediationDescription:
### AWS

1. Confirm whether the affected instance runs directly on EC2 or inside a container host that intentionally needs more than one metadata hop.
2. For standard EC2 instances, set the IMDS response hop limit to 1.
3. Run:

~~~sh
aws ec2 modify-instance-metadata-options --instance-id $instanceID --http-put-response-hop-limit 1 --http-endpoint enabled
~~~

4. Update launch templates and Auto Scaling groups so replacement instances inherit the same setting.
5. Recycle instances launched from old templates where needed.
6. Re-sync and confirm the metadata hop limit is 1 on the affected instances.

Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/configuring-instance-metadata-options.html
